displayMetadata.name: PREPARATION
displayMetadata.value:
1. Combine 1600ml Water with 200g Golden Chef Prawn Noodles Paste
2. Bring mixture to boil
3. Serve hot with blanched yellow noodles. Customise your bowl with your preferred toppings, such as tender blanched prawns and blanched vegetables. Enjoy!

1. In a pot combine 1600ml Water with 200g Golden Chef Prawn Noodles Paste
2. Bring the mixture to a boil
3. Serve hot with blanched yellow noodles. Add your favorite toppings: such as tender prawns , vegetables and more. Enjoy!

displayMetadata.name: STORAGE INFORMATION
displayMetadata.value: Store in a cool and dry place

displayMetadata.name: INGREDIENTS
displayMetadata.value:
Palm Oil [Palm Olein, Anti-Oxidant (E320)], Onion, Sugar, Dried Prawns, Salt, Galangal, Flavour Enhancers (E621, E635), Garlic, Chilli, Lemongrass, Thickener [Water, Thickener (E1442)], Prawn Flavouring [Synthetic Flavouring, Natural Flavouring, Maltodextrin, Stabiliser (E1450), Flavouring Agents (E1518, E1520), Acidity Regulator (E260), Anti-Oxidant (E307)] and Colour (E160C).

Processed In A Facility That Also Processes Cereals Containing Gluten, Crustacean, Egg, Fish, Peanuts, Soybeans, Milk, Tree Nuts and Nuts Products.

Cooking method:
1. Mix chicken meat (400g) and Golden Chef Singapore Chicken Curry Paste (250g) in a pot and stir fry over medium heat for 5 minutes
2. Add in water (100ml), potato cubes (150g, peeled and cubed) and curry leaves (30g)
3. Cook over medium heat for 10 to 15 minutes or until all ingredients are cooked
4. Ready to serve

metaData.Key Information: A flavourful tomato and chilli base combining the perfect balance of sweet, spicy and tangy notes, capturing the essence of the iconic Singapore Chilli Crab dish. This aromatic ready-to-cook paste is perfect for recreating restaurant-quality chilli crab at home or adding a delicious twist to your favourite seafood dishes. An exclusive recipe by Celebrity Chef Eric Teo!
metaData.Nutritional Data:
AttributesPer Serving (125g)
Energy 361kcal
Protein 4.1g
Total Fat 29.1g
Saturated Fat 11.8g
Trans Fat 0.0g
Cholesterol 33mg
Carbohydrate 20.8g
Total Sugar 17.9g
Dietary Fibre 2.6g
Sodium 1261mg
metaData.Preparation:
Preparation (serves 2 to 3)
• 1 packet Golden Chef Chef Series Singapore Chilli Crab Paste 250g
• 800g Crab
• 1 Large egg, beaten
• 100ml Water

Cooking Method
1. Mix Golden Chef Chef Series Singapore Chilli Crab Paste and water in a pot and bring it to a boil. Set aside.
2. In a separate wok, add the crab pieces and fry over high heat till fragrant.
3. Add in the chilli crab sauce and coat the crab evenly.
4. Gently add the beaten egg and stir well over medium heat till cooked.
5. Ready to serve.

- Allergen Advice: Contains Wheat, Soy and Crustacean.

Processed In A Facility That Also Processes Cereals Containing Gluten, Crustacean, Egg, Fish, Peanut, Soybeans, Milk, Tree Nuts and Nuts Products.
metaData.Key Information: A flavourful blend that captures the essence of the popular dish of Malay origin - Mee Rebus. This thick, nutty gravy is laced with Chinese style salted soya bean paste and a medley of spices. An aromatic ready-to-cook paste featuring a perfect mix of sweet, savoury & slightly tangy spicy notes with a velvety smooth texture. Great for coating Yellow Noodles complete with toppings like hard-boiled eggs, tofu and chilli.
metaData.Nutritional Data:
AttributesPer Serving (125g)
Energy 354kcal
Protein 6.9g
Total Fat 21.8g
Saturated Fat 7.6g
Trans Fat 0.0g
Cholesterol 31mg
Carbohydrate 32.8g
Total Sugar 29.9g
Dietary Fibre 3.3g
Sodium 1085mg
metaData.Preparation:
Preparation (serves 2)
- 1 packet Golden Chef Mee Rebus Paste 250g
- 220g of Yellow noodles
- 100g of Bean Sprouts
- 2 Hard Boiled Eggs, sliced in half
- 60g of Fried Beancurd, diced cut
- 6 Medium Prawns
- 2 Lime, sliced half
- 20g of Green Chilli
- 10g of Fried Shallot
- 330ml of Water
- 12g of Corn Starch with 20ml of Water (as corn starch slurry)

Cooking Method
1. Mix Golden Chef Mee Rebus Paste and water in a pot and bring it to a boil.
2. In a separate bowl, add corn starch into water and mix well.
3. Add in the mixture slowly to thicken the gravy in the pot. Lower the heat and simmer for 5 mins.
4. Deep-fry beancurd and blanch yellow noodles, bean sprouts and prawns separately in boiling water. Drain and set aside.
5. Portion into individual bowls and pour hot gravy over. Top it off with garnishes. Ready to serve.

• Allergen Advice: Contains Milk, Soy and Wheat.

Processed In A Facility That Also Processes Cereals Containing Gluten, Crustacean, Egg, Fish, Peanut, Soybeans, Milk, Tree Nuts and Nuts Products."
metaData.Key Information: A savoury blend designed to capture the essence of the beloved Singaporean dish - Hainanese chicken rice. This robust and aromatic chicken stock base brings together the comforting and savoury flavours of poached chicken, fragrant rice and real nuances of ginger aftertaste with a satisfyingly slick mouthfeel. A ready-to-cook paste that is great as a marinade or seasoning for other dishes to impart its distinctive and rich flavours.
metaData.Nutritional Data:
AttributesPer Serving (39g)
Energy 121kcal
Protein 1.1g
Total Fat 11.7g
Saturated Fat 4.3g
Trans Fat 0.0g
Cholesterol 14mg
Carbohydrate 2.8g
Total Sugar 2.0g
Dietary Fibre 1.0g
Sodium 634mg
metaData.Preparation:
Preparation (serves 2)
• 1 packet Golden Chef Singapore Hainanese Chicken Rice Paste 80g
• 200g Long grain rice
• 230ml Water

Cooking Method
1. Wash rice once and drain well.
2. Pour rice and Golden Chef Hainanese Chicken Rice Paste into rice cooker and mix well. Add water. You may adjust the amount of water for different types of rice at your own preference. Stir well and cook the rice.
3. When rice is cooked, let it rest at 'Keep Warm' setting for 10-15 minutes.
4. Fluff rice. Ready to serve.
